title: Guard/hook toggles persist to the gitignored local overlay, never the tracked config (D2)
action: Per CONTEXT D2 (docs/history/intake-gate-git-exemption/CONTEXT.md). This is the defect that let a temporary safety lift reach a commit (incident a7d2069). `bee config set` / `config unset` must route the `guards.*` and `hooks.*` namespaces to `.bee/config.local.json` (the gitignored overlay that ALREADY exists — see .bee/bin/lib/state.mjs:1261-1310, hardening-8), never to the tracked `.bee/config.json`. Read precedence stays exactly as today (overlay wins over tracked), so an existing tracked toggle keeps being honored — read compatibility is required, but such a value is never WRITTEN back to the tracked file again; per CONTEXT's open question, do NOT auto-migrate/auto-edit the tracked file, and surface a one-line warning when a guards.*/hooks.* key is found in the tracked config so the human can move it deliberately. Other namespaces (commands, models, lanes, gate_bypass, ...) keep their current home — do not widen this. RED-FIRST: write the checks first (find the config test home — likely skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_config_validate.mjs or a sibling; follow the standalone convention with mkdtemp fixture roots), run red, record `cells verify --id ige-1 --passed false`, then implement. Checks: `config set --key guards.idle_gate --value false` writes ONLY .bee/config.local.json and leaves .bee/config.json byte-identical; the value is honored on read; `config unset` removes it from the overlay; a pre-existing guards.* value in the tracked config is still honored on read AND produces the warning; a non-guard/hook key (e.g. commands.verify) still writes to the tracked config as before. Mirror to .bee/bin, render + release_manifest --write/--check, TARGETED verify only (the config suites) — no full chain (the orchestrator runs it at wave close).

truths: config set/unset of guards.*/hooks.* touches ONLY .bee/config.local.json; the tracked .bee/config.json stays byte-identical
the overlay value is honored on read (existing precedence unchanged)
a guards.*/hooks.* key already sitting in the tracked config is still honored AND warned about, never auto-edited
RED-FIRST failing run recorded before implementation
prohibitions: Never auto-migrate or auto-edit the tracked .bee/config.json
Do not widen the routing beyond guards.* and hooks.*

trace.outcome: guards.*/hooks.* toggles now persist only to the gitignored .bee/config.local.json — the tracked config can no longer carry a machine-local safety lift
trace.verify_output:
PASS  config set --key guards.* --value ... (no --local) writes ONLY .bee/config.local.json; leaves an absent .bee/config.json absent
PASS  config set --key guards.* --value ... leaves a PRE-EXISTING tracked config.json byte-identical
PASS  a guards.* value set without --local is honored on read via readConfig() (overlay precedence unchanged)
PASS  config unset --key guards.* (no --local) removes it from the overlay only, tracked config.json untouched
PASS  hooks.* keys route the same as guards.* (local overlay only, never tracked)
PASS  a guards.* value already sitting in the TRACKED config.json is still honored on read AND produces a warning; never auto-migrated
PASS  config unset --key guards.* never auto-edits a pre-existing TRACKED value (only the overlay is ever touched)
PASS  a non-guard/hook key (e.g. commands.verify) still writes to the tracked config.json as before

8 passed, 0 failed
